skill.name: claude-plugin
skill.description: Adds repo-map MCP guidance for Claude Code, including symbol search, file symbol lookup, repository indexing, and project context files.
skill.summary: Use repo-map MCP tools to search code symbols and inspect project structure faster.
skill.author: ChipFlow

security_audit.summary: Static command-execution findings in SKILL.md are mostly Markdown examples and inline code, so those are false positives. However, plugin.json configures Claude Code to launch a local MCP server with the current project root, and SKILL.md documents indexing and querying project files through a local SQLite repo map. No prompt injection or confirmed data exfiltration was found, but the executable MCP server and project-wide filesystem access make this high risk for a community package.

high_findings.title: MCP Server Launches Local Executable
high_findings.description: plugin.json configures an MCP server that runs uv with a repo-map server path and passes the current working directory as PROJECT_ROOT. This is expected for an MCP plugin, but it means installing the community package grants executable code a view of the active project.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The mcpServers command and args are explicit in plugin.json, and PROJECT_ROOT is set from the current working directory. The risk is clear even though the referenced server implementation is not present in the scanned three-file package.

medium_findings.title: Project Filesystem Indexing and Source Retrieval
medium_findings.description: SKILL.md documents automatic use of .claude/repo-map.db, repository indexing, symbol lookup, and source retrieval through MCP tools. This is core functionality, but it can expose project structure and source snippets to the MCP server and logs.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documentation directly states that the tools query or create .claude/repo-map.db and retrieve symbol content. This appears intentional, so the concern is data exposure and least-privilege scope rather than confirmed abuse.

low_findings.title: Repository URL Findings Are Metadata Only
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL findings in plugin.json and marketplace.json are repository metadata. No evidence found that these URLs are used for runtime network requests or data exfiltration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both locations are JSON repository fields, not executable request code. The files contain no fetch, curl, webhook, or outbound transmission behavior.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The static weak-cryptography findings point to descriptive text, not cryptographic APIs or hashing logic. No evidence found of MD5, SHA1, weak ciphers, or password hashing implementation in the cited files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are descriptions for context tools and do not contain cryptographic operations. This makes the scanner labels highly likely to be keyword-based false positives.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backtick Command Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The SKILL.md backtick findings are Markdown code fences, inline parameter names, examples, and schema labels. No evidence found of Ruby shell backtick execution in executable source code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is documentation, and the cited patterns are Markdown formatting rather than runtime Ruby code. This still required review because the plugin separately configures MCP execution in plugin.json.

dangerous_patterns.title: Executable MCP Server Configuration
dangerous_patterns.description: The plugin declares an MCP server command that runs uv against a Python server path. Marketplace review should require the referenced server code before publication.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command configuration is explicit and will run when the MCP server starts. The security impact depends on server code that is not available in the scanned package.

dangerous_patterns.title: Local Project Index Database
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill stores or reads project index data under .claude/repo-map.db and logs MCP activity under .claude/logs. These hidden project files may contain sensitive project metadata.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The documented paths and log contents are explicit. The data appears local, but it can still expose repository structure and tool activity to anyone with project access.

content.value_statement: Large repositories make symbol navigation slow and repetitive. This skill guides Claude Code to use repo-map MCP tools for faster code search and project context.
actual_capabilities: Explains when to use repo-map MCP tools instead of text search.
Documents symbol search by name pattern, kind, and result limit.
Documents file-level symbol lookup for a relative project path.
Documents exact symbol content retrieval for functions, classes, and methods.
Describes repo-map indexing status, waiting behavior, and manual reindexing.
limitations: Requires the repo-map MCP server to be installed and restarted before use.
Only documents Python, C++, and Rust symbol indexing support.
Does not include the MCP server implementation in the scanned package.
Creates or reads local .claude project files that may contain repository metadata.

use_cases.title: Find Symbols Quickly
use_cases.description: Search for functions, classes, and methods by pattern without scanning entire files manually.

use_cases.title: Review File Structure
use_cases.description: List symbols defined in a file before reviewing implementation details or related changes.

use_cases.title: Maintain Repository Context
use_cases.description: Use generated project manifests and repo maps to keep Claude Code aware of repository structure.

prompt_templates.title: Search for a Symbol
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the repo-map tools to find functions matching parse_* in this repository. Summarize the likely entry points.

prompt_templates.title: Inspect a File
prompt_templates.prompt: Use get_file_symbols for src/models/user.py and explain the main classes and functions before reading the file.

prompt_templates.title: Trace an Implementation
prompt_templates.prompt: Find the exact User.save symbol, retrieve its content, and identify validation, database, and error-handling behavior.

prompt_templates.title: Refresh Project Index
prompt_templates.prompt: Check repo-map status, reindex if stale, wait for completion, and then compare handler symbols across Python and Rust files.

output_examples.output: A concise list of matching handler symbols, their files, and a short note about which ones appear to control login flow.

output_examples.input: Explain what symbols are defined in the user model file.
output_examples.output: A structured summary of classes, methods, functions, and their line locations, followed by suggested next files to inspect.

output_examples.input: Check whether the repo-map index is ready.
output_examples.output: A status summary showing whether indexing is idle, running, complete, failed, or stale, with the recommended next action.

best_practices: Restart Claude Code after plugin installation or update so MCP tools become available.
Use repo-map tools for symbol names, then read full files only when deeper context is needed.
Review generated .claude files before sharing a project workspace.
anti_patterns: Do not use symbol search for comments, strings, or non-code text patterns.
Do not assume unsupported languages are indexed with complete symbol coverage.
Do not publish repositories with sensitive generated .claude files included.

faq.question: What does this skill help Claude Code do?
faq.answer: It guides Claude Code to use repo-map MCP tools for symbol search, file symbol lookup, source retrieval, and index status checks.

faq.question: Does it replace normal file reading?
faq.answer: No. It helps locate relevant symbols first, then normal file reading can provide full context.

faq.question: Why must Claude Code restart after installation?
faq.answer: Claude Code starts MCP servers when a session starts, so new plugin server settings require a new session.

faq.question: Which languages are documented?
faq.answer: The skill documentation lists Python, C++, and Rust as supported symbol languages.

faq.question: Where does the repo map store project data?
faq.answer: The documentation references local .claude files, including repo-map.db, repo-map.md, project-manifest.json, and logs.

faq.question: Is it safe for private repositories?
faq.answer: Use caution. The skill indexes local project structure and may record metadata in hidden .claude files and logs.
